restaurant_name: Perimeter Post - Rreal Tacos - Sandy Springs
blurb:
This place absolutely lives up to all the buzz on social media. The atmosphere is vibrant and fun, perfect for girls' night out or when you want good Mexican food with friends. The service is genuinely helpful and they know their menu well.

Start with the Esquite because it's packed with flavor and gets the table sharing. The Pineapple Guava pitcher is refreshing and perfectly balanced between sweet and strong. The vibe is lively but not too loud, and they're open late which is great for weekend dinners that turn into longer nights.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM

restaurant_name: Perimeter Post - Hyderabad House Atlanta - Biryani Place
blurb:
This is where I go when I'm craving real Indian food and want generous portions that'll feed the whole family. It's not fancy but the food is authentic, and they serve everything family-style so you can share. Perfect for when you want to introduce the kids to Indian food or need a satisfying weeknight dinner that's different from the usual.

The biryani is their specialty and for good reason. It comes in a huge portion with tender meat and fragrant rice that's actually worth the hype. The colorful, casual setting makes it comfortable for families, and the staff is helpful if you're not sure what to order.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:30 PM, Saturday: 9:00 AM – 11:30 PM, Sunday: 9:00 AM – 9:30 PM

restaurant_name: East Cobb Connect - Marietta Square Market
blurb:
This food hall is perfect when your group can't agree on what to eat. It has about twenty different vendors under one roof, from tacos to sushi to barbecue. The kids can get pizza while the adults try something more adventurous. It's right near the Marietta Square, so you can walk around downtown before or after.

I usually end up at the taco place or the Asian fusion spot, but there's honestly something for everyone. The seating is communal style with big tables, so it has a social vibe. It's especially good for lunch when you're exploring downtown Marietta or want to meet friends without committing to one specific cuisine.

Parking can be tricky during busy times since you're right downtown, but it's worth the short walk. Great for families, casual dates, or when you want options without driving to multiple places.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Mon-Thu & Sun 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
This is the place we hit when nothing else is open or when we want that classic diner experience without driving to downtown Atlanta. It's been a Marietta institution forever, with that retro neon sign you can spot from Cobb Parkway. Perfect for late night cravings, early morning breakfast before a flight, or when you have picky eaters in the group since the menu has literally everything.

The Greek specialties are surprisingly good here. I always go for the gyro platter or the spanakopita when I want something different from the usual diner fare. Their breakfast is solid too and portions are huge, so don't order too much your first time. The coffee flows freely and the waitstaff has been there forever.

Being open 24/7 is their biggest selling point. It's saved us more times than I can count when we needed food at weird hours or wanted breakfast at 2am.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

restaurant_name: East Cobb Connect - Douceur De France - Bakery & Brunch
blurb:
This little French bakery is a hidden gem that makes you feel like you're in Paris instead of Marietta. The pastries are legit French, made fresh daily by actual French bakers. It's bright and cheerful inside, perfect for a leisurely weekend brunch or grabbing coffee and a croissant on your way to work.

Their quiche is what I order every time I go for brunch, and the petits fours are beautiful little works of art. The baguettes are crusty on the outside and soft inside, just like they should be. Their coffee is strong and European-style, not the watered-down American stuff.

Just know they close early on Sundays and aren't open late any day. It's worth planning your visit around their hours because this level of authentic French baking is rare in the suburbs.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM

restaurant_name: East Cobb Connect - Jerusalem Bakery & Grill
blurb:
This place has become our go-to for Middle Eastern food that doesn't break the bank. It's in a strip mall off Franklin Gateway, but don't let the outside fool you. The food is fresh and authentic, and the portions are generous enough that we often split an entree.

The chicken shawarma is what I order every time, and their falafel is crispy on the outside and fluffy inside. My wife loves their veggie options, especially the hummus and tabbouleh. Everything comes with warm pita bread that they make fresh. The baklava is worth saving room for if you have a sweet tooth.

It's casual and family-friendly, good for a quick lunch or an easy dinner when you want something different from the usual chain restaurants in the area.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

restaurant_name: East Cobb Connect - Rio Steakhouse & Bakery
blurb:
If you've never done Brazilian all-you-can-eat barbecue, this is a great place to try it without the fancy prices of the big chains. The servers come around with different cuts of meat on skewers, and you flip a little card to green when you want more or red when you need a break. It's fun for groups and perfect when you're really hungry.

The cheese bread they bring out first is addictive, so pace yourself. The picanha steak is my favorite cut they serve, and the feijoada stew is hearty and authentic if you want something different. They also do breakfast and have a bakery section with Brazilian pastries that are worth checking out.

It's in the Powers Ferry shopping area, so parking is easy. Good for celebrations, date nights, or when you want to try something new with friends who are up for an adventure.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Mon-Thu & Sun 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM

restaurant_name: Perimeter Post - Ray's on the River
blurb:
This is our go-to for special occasions when we want to impress out-of-town visitors or celebrate something important. The setting right on the Chattahoochee River is gorgeous, especially if you can snag a window table. It's definitely a splurge but worth it for anniversaries, business dinners, or when your parents are visiting.

The Southern seafood here is elevated but not pretentious. Whatever fresh fish they're featuring is usually your best bet, and the service is the kind where they actually remember what you ordered without writing it down. The intimate atmosphere makes it feel special without being stuffy.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM, 5:00 – 9:00 PM

restaurant_name: Perimeter Post - Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
I was skeptical about another pizza place until I tried this one. The crust is thin and actually crispy, not floppy like most places around here. It's in the Blue Stone shopping area and perfect for a casual dinner when you want good pizza that's not just okay pizza.

They use ingredients imported from Italy and you can taste the difference. The margherita is simple but done right, and they have creative toppings if you want something different. Good for date nights that aren't too formal or family dinners where everyone can agree on pizza.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM

blurb:
My girlfriend insisted I'd love this place for our dinner out and she was absolutely right. This has become one of my top 5 dinner spots in Atlanta. The Persian food is incredible and the cocktails are creative and well-made. Perfect for a special dinner or when you want to try something different.

The chicken shish kabobs with rice with lentils and raisins are amazing, and definitely get the hummus to start. The wings are surprisingly good too. Save room for the tiramisu because it's worth it. The whole experience feels special without being stuffy.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
